summary: This is a brief definitional entry that establishes 'Hollywood Principle' as an alternative name for Inversion of Control. The post serves as a cross-reference in Fowler's bliki (blog-wiki hybrid), directing readers to the fuller explanation of the concept. The Hollywood Principle name comes from the phrase 'Don't call us, we'll call you' - reflecting how frameworks call your code rather than your code calling the framework.
key_points: Hollywood Principle and Inversion of Control are synonymous terms
The entry serves as a navigational aid in Fowler's interconnected bliki format
The name alludes to Hollywood casting calls: 'Don't call us, we'll call you'
key_insight: The Hollywood Principle ('Don't call us, we'll call you') is simply another name for the Inversion of Control pattern.
